name: Wyatt Ruther
abstract: Wyatt Robert "Bull" Ruther (February 5, 1923, Pittsburgh – October 31, 1999, San Francisco) was an American jazz double-bassist.

concerts.label: All-Star Jazz Concert and Recording Session

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: the bass player, Bull Ruther, decided to go back to Seattle... So they needed a bass player. I said, 'I can do that book too.' ... And that was the beginning of it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Herb Barman and Bull Ruther. What a wild rhythm section that was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Vernon Alley put a little group together with Wardell in it and Gerald Wiggins. Yeah. Wiggins had gotten out of the service. Gerald Wiggins and Wyatt Ruther, Bull Ruther, the bass player... Anyhow this was around 1947 so Vernon had put this group together. And so he had Wiggins and Wardell, himself,
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Vernon Alley put a little group together with Wardell in it and Gerald Wiggins. Yeah. Wiggins had gotten out of the service. Gerald Wiggins and Wyatt Ruther, Bull Ruther, the bass player... He had this quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: Bull Ruther, a wonderful bass player, he lived in the projects. We’d have barbeques.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Richard Wyands
about_quotes.evidence: Wyatt Ruther and I, we went to night school to try to learn French.
about_quotes.doc_title: Richard Wyands interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002
